msgs.text:
I treated the prompt‚Äôs submission rules and the 61-turn prior log as constraints and proposals, not as proof. The problem asks for a profinite generators-and-relations presentation of GQ2, in the special format where ŌÉ,ŌĄ are tame generators, ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2 is implicit, later generators lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are given modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves one serious unrefuted candidate after many finite-quotient failures of earlier scalar/plain-ŌÄ2/fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 variants.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ, ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ‚ąíő∑ is immaterial in this word, since ŌĄ‚ąíő∑ is only evaluated on prime-to-2 tame inertia.The late prior turns‚Äô two stable algebraic conventions remain correct when stated as equations rather than informal fractions. For the sign-cover coefficient, if t is the deck involution of V=kerőļ, and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character, thenDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j satisfies (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąít,soDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.

Thus Da is radical, and the coefficient from x‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. This is the solid reason nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0 up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The elimination polynomial should be defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.

Then, with h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one has for every nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).

Since s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm) for f=2a m, the integer 2s+1 is coprime to f. Hence the trivial block remains nonsingular. With these conventions, the prior candidate-side conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÉZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÉZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain coherent.My new contribution is a further gauge correction to the first-IA obstruction package.The prior turns correctly reframed the first marked IA obstruction as an arithmetic-minus-candidate class in a homogeneous difference space, not as raw coordinates of one lift. For even f=2n, the mixed part depends only on the anti-cyclotomic divisor d=gcd(e,2n+1),and is naturally modeled byMd=F2[Cd]‚ČÉF2 [T]/(Td‚ąí1),up to two universal square directions b[2]and S[2]. The prior log then said that the gauge-invariant content is support in the Wedderburn decomposition of Md. That is true only after a tame inertia generator has been fixed.There is an additional tame-inertia generator gauge. Replacing the tame generator byŌĄ‚Ä≤=ŌĄu,u‚ąą(Z/e)√ó,preserves the tame relation because(ŌĄ‚Ä≤)ŌÉ=(ŌĄu)ŌÉ=ŌĄ2u=(ŌĄ‚Ä≤)2.

On the anti-cyclotomic index set, the involutionőīj=ŌĄjŌÉn is rewritten asőīj=(ŌĄ‚Ä≤)u‚ąí1jŌÉn.

Thus, on the mixed residual

R(T)=j‚ąąZ/d‚ąĎrjTj‚ąąF2[Cd],the tame-generator gauge acts by the semilinear automorphism

R(T)‚üľR(Tu‚ąí1).This must be combined with the already-noted normal-basis unit gauge and Frobenius-lift gauge. The effective first-IA gauge group on the mixed residual is thereforeF2[Cd]√ó‚čä(Z/d)√ó,where F2[Cd]√óacts by multiplication, and (Z/d)√óacts by T‚Ü¶Tu.Consequently, named irreducible factors in the Wedderburn decomposition are not intrinsically labeled until ŌĄ is fixed. More precisely, for each exact order c‚ą£d, the irreducible factors of ő¶c (T) over F2 correspond to cosets (Z/c)√ó/‚ü®2‚ü©.The tame-generator gauge acts transitively on those cosets. So the intrinsic first-IA mixed obstruction is not a named support subset in the Wedderburn decomposition, but its orbit under this (Z/d)√ó-action.This does not change Turn 61‚Äôs proposed next test (e,f)=(9,6). For d=9,F2[C9]‚ČÉF2‚äēF4‚äēF64,and each exact-order level 1,3,9 has only one irreducible factor. Thus the projectors from Turn 61 are genuinely canonical up to all these gauges:E1=j=0‚ąĎ8Tj,E3=T+T2+T4+T5+T7+T8,E9=T3+T6.

The zero test remains:E1R=E3R=E9R=0,together with zero in the two square directions. In coefficient form, this is:j=0‚ąĎ8rj=0,r0+r3+r6=r1+r4+r7=r2+r5+r8,andr0=r3=r6,r1=r4=r7,r2=r5=r8.But for later tests this extra gauge matters. For d=17,F2[C17]‚ČÉF2‚äēF256‚äēF256,and the two primitive 17-components are exchanged by the tame-generator gauge. A residual supported in the first primitive factor and one supported in the second are the same obstruction unless ŌĄ has been fixed. For d=65, the primitive 65-part has four degree-12 factors; their named labels likewise have meaning only after a ŌĄ-gauge choice.So the next arithmetic comparison should be stated as follows. ForL=Q2(ő∂9,ŌĖ),ŌĖ9=2,fix all three gauges: a tame inertia generator ŌĄ, a Frobenius lift ŌÉ, and a normal-basis generator for the regular Kummer summand. Then compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate first-IA residual(ŌĶb,ŌĶS;R(T))‚ąąF22‚äēF2[C9],and test it with E1,E3,E9. The target is still the zero tuple. If the result is nonzero, it is a genuine first-IA obstruction to the live word; if it is zero, the candidate survives the first primitive-9 anti-cyclotomic test.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I preserved the stable conventionsDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚ąís.New this turn: the anti-cyclotomic mixed residual has an additional tame-inertia generator gauge. Replacing ŌĄ by ŌĄu acts on R(T)‚ąąF2[Cd] by R(T)‚Ü¶R(Tu‚ąí1). Thus named Wedderburn-factor supports are intrinsic only after fixing ŌĄ; otherwise they are defined up to (Z/d)√ó-orbits. The (9,6) projector test is unaffected because d=9 has one factor at each exact-order level.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient remains an arithmetic comparison, or a dyadic formation theorem, proving that the actual local Galois action has the same candidate-normalized affine origins over every finite tame subextension. The first-IA obstruction package is now better normalized, but the arithmetic residual has not been computed.

NEXT: For L=Q2(ő∂9,ŌĖ), ŌĖ9=2, fix the tame inertia generator, Frobenius lift, and normal-basis gauge. Compute the arithmetic-minus-candidate first-IA residual (ŌĶb,ŌĶS;R(T)), then apply E1,E3,E9. The target is zero in F22‚äēF2‚äēF4‚äēF64.[BREAKTHROUGH]
